mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, enabling state-of-the-art inference performance on various hardware accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the llm-d command-line interface to interact with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This installs the CLI tool for managing llm-d.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file with the new cluster context.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that all nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
Deploy llm-d using Helm to manage the application lifecycle.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> This installs llm-d in a dedicated namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the necessary configurations for autoscaling.
> This YAML file sets up monitoring for llm-d.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
Remove llm-d and all associated resources. Warning: This will delete all data managed by llm-d.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command removes the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Backup existing configurations, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade llm-d to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the container image could not be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Check the image name in the deployment YAML and ensure it matches the available images.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when a pod fails to start repeatedly, often due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved autoscaling capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d', indicating that all pods are running successfully.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
